risk_title: Project Implementation Suspended Due to Political Instability
risk_summary: The Afghanistan Resilience Trust Fund (ARTF) and project financing have been canceled, and IDA grants remain suspended due to the extraordinary political situation in Afghanistan, preventing effective project implementation.
evidence_quote: The Afghanistan Resilience Trust Fund (ARTF) and any other trust fund in the project was canceled based on the consideration that the project cannot be effectively implemented under the current extraordinary situation in Afghanistan. Undisbursed IDA grants under this project will remain suspended until a decision is made by the World Bank.

risk_title: Political and Governance Risk Remains High
risk_summary: Political and governance risks are rated as High at both approval and current assessment, indicating persistent fragility in the government's ability to support project implementation.
evidence_quote: Risk Category: Political and Governance - Rating at Approval: High, Previous Rating: High, Current Rating: High

risk_summary: Macroeconomic risks remain rated as High, reflecting broader economic fragility in Afghanistan that undermines project sustainability and implementation capacity.
evidence_quote: Risk Category: Macroeconomic - Rating at Approval: High, Previous Rating: High, Current Rating: High

risk_summary: Only 8.1% of the $52.5 million IDA credit has been disbursed ($4.37M) as of December 2023, with $49.53M remaining undisbursed, indicating severe implementation constraints linked to the political situation.
evidence_quote: Project Loan/Credit/TF: P172109 IDA-D5210 - Disbursed: 4.37, Undisbursed: 49.53, % Disbursed: 8.1%
